def: part. Mixing; mixed; fraught with (used in comp. e.g. mihr-āmez, fraught with friendship); — s.m. Mixture (see next)
s.f. Mixture, mixing; capability of mingling; sociableness, bonhomic; mixed feelings; mingled motives; lack of purity or sincerity or disinterestedness; duplicity; alloy, adulteration.
s.f. Water of life, nectar; water, rain.
adj. Common, &c. ( = ʻāmm or ʻām, q.v.).

ety: for A. عامٍ ʻāmiṉ, act. part. of عمي 'to be or become blind'
def: adj. Blind, ignorant.

ety: v.n. fr. عمق 'to be deep'; see ʻumuq
def: adj. Deep, profound (syn. gahrā).

s.m. A worker, doer, an operator; an agent; a governor, ruler, administrator; an intendant of finance, a collector of revenues; an official, officer, functionary; — a necromancer, conjurer; — adj. (in Gram.) Governing.

ety: for A. عاملة, fem. of ʻāmil
def: s.m. (in Gram.), A governing word.
adj. Common, universal; complete, perfect, full; abundant, numerous; reaching to everything, extending to all, all-comprehensive.

adj. Trusted in; trusty, trustworthy, confidential, faithful; — s.m. A person intrusted with an office, or with affairs; a commissioner, trustee, guardian, umpire, arbitrator, investigator; law officer; inspector; superintendent; an officer appointed to make rough surveys after the native method; an officer employed by Government to take charge of an estate and collect the revenues, or to investigate and report their amount
adv. Amen, so be it; — intj. God keep us!
s.f. The office of an amīn; — trust, guardianship, custody; security; — commission; deputation
part. One who orders, commander, ruler.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آمر (āmir), from Arabic آمِر (ʔāmir).

def: s.m. Commander, governor, lord, prince, noble, chief; a person of rank or distinction; a gentleman
subs: s.m. Chief of the nobles; lord of lords (a title given by Eastern princes to their prime ministers)
s.m. The commander of the fleet, an admiral
adj. (f. -ī) Of noble birth; — s.m. One of noble birth or family; a king's son, a prince; — amīr-zādī, s.f. Princess
s.m. Commander of the faithful (a title assumed by the ḵẖalīfas or Caliphs).

ety: for A. عامرة, fem. of عامر ʻāmir, act. part. of عمر 'to be peopled,' &c.
def: adj. Peopled, inhabited, colonized, cultivated, in a flourishing state (syn. ābād); abundant; rich, fertile; royal, imperial.
adj. Like a noble; noble, lordly, princely, grand, stately; — adv. Nobly, &c.
adj. Of or relating to an amīr; princely, grand, magnificent; — s.f. The rank or office of an amīr; principality, lordship; sovereignty, sway; dignity, nobility, grandeur, greatness
subs: v.n. To exercise sway or authority, to rule; to lord it, to live like a prince
v.n. To revel in grandeur and luxury; to live in the enjoyment of luxurious ease; to neglect one's affairs.

def: adj. Like a noble; noble, lordly, princely, grand, stately; — adv. Nobly, &c.
s.m. Flesh, food; enjoyment, an object of enjoyment, a pleasing object, lure, bait; an object of prey.
adj. Immeasureable; unmeasured; undefined.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اُمَّت (ummat), borrowed from Arabic أُمَّة (ʔumma).

def: adj. Not effaced; ineffaceable, indelible; indestructible, imperishable, indissoluble; irreversible, irrevocable; immutable, unchangeable, unvarying, firm, steadfast.
s.f. = amī, q.v.
adj. Undying, imperishable.
adj. (compar. & superl. of majd), More, or most, glorious.

ety: S. अमुक+redup. formed from S. base त
def: s.m. This one and that one, small and great, every one (promiscuously); a nobody (a vague term of contempt used in speaking of a person); trifling, trifles, baubles; idle talk, prating.

ety: S. अमुक+redup. formed from S. base त
def: s.m. This one and that one, small and great, every one (promiscuously); a nobody (a vague term of contempt used in speaking of a person); trifling, trifles, baubles; idle talk, prating.
s.m. The plant Emblic Myrobalan, Phyllanthus emblica (commonly called آنولا āṅwlā).
adj. Sour, acid; — s.m. Sourness, acidity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian امل (amal), from Arabic أَمَل (ʔamal).

ety: prob. for A. عملة ʻamilat; or fr. ʻamal
def: s.m. Work, &c. ( = ʻamal); a thing done; premises, buildings, &c.; materials (of a dilapidated house).
s.m. pl. (of ʻamla), Public officials; subordinate officers, clerks; managers.
s.f. pl. (of milk), Possessions, goods, estates, property, lands.
adj. Clean, pure; clear, bright; — s.m. Globe-amaranth, Gomphræna globosa.
s.f. Sourness, acidity.
s.f. The tamarind tree. (See imlī.)

ety: act. part. of عمّ 'to be common,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Common, general, universal; public; vulgar, popular; plebeian; ordinary; generally or universally comprehensive (of -se), not restricted, including, inclusive (of); — the vulgar, the common people, the mass
subs: adj. Generally intelligible; popular (as a treatise)
adj. (f. -ī), Of or relating to the public, or to the masses; public
s.m. pl. The common people, &c. ( = ʻām)
High and low, gentle and simple, rich and poor.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَامّ (āmm), borrowed from Arabic عَامّ (ʕāmm).

def: s.m. A father s brother, paternal uncle
conj. But, moreover, nevertheless, however, yet.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اما (ammā), from Arabic أَمَّا (ʔammā).

def: s.f. Mother ( = mā, māṅ); — intj. (used by women), My dear! darling!
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اما (ammā), from Arabic أَمَّا (ʔammā).

def: s.f. = ammā, q.v.

ety: P. انبار ambār or A. amār
def: s.m. Heap, pile. (The word would appear to be peculiar to the Panjāb.)
(for A. امارة ammārat, fem. of ammār), adj. m.f. Wont to command; imperious, domineering, headstrong, obstinate. (See nafs-i-ammāra, s.v. nafs.)
s.f. = amāvas, q.v.
s.f. One of the ten kinds of wounds compensated by a fine.
s.m. Father's brother, paternal uncle (cf. ḵẖālū, fr. ḵẖāl).
s.m. Security, safety; tranquillity, peace
subs: amn o amān, s.m. Security and tranquillity; — adv. In safety and peace
v.t. To keep in safety, &c., to take care of, preserve
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَمْن (ʔamn).

def: v.n. (prov.) = ānā, q.v.
s.m. Confronting, opposition; front; — adj. Facing, opposite
subs: adv. Opposite, face to face, in front
v.t. To confront, face; to place before or in front; to bring face to face; to put side by side, to compare.

def: see s.v. āmnā-sāmnā.
s.f. Security, safety; repose ( = amn); — a place of safety.
s.m. Diffusive perfume, odour, fragrance.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit आमोद (āmoda)

def: adj. Unerring, unfailing; effectual; infallible; genuine, real; productive, fruitful.

ety: perf. part. of āmoḵẖtan; ā+rt. Zend muć = S. मुच्
def: part. adj. Taught, learned; — s.m. That which has been learned; an old lesson
subs: To read over old lessons, to revise.

def: adj. Priceless, invaluable, unpurchaseable, very rare or precious; super-excellent, beautiful, lovely, exquisite.

ety: S. आम्र+क+dim. aff. र; Pr. अम्बो+लको; — o-lā is a com. dim. aff. in the modern Aryan tongues
def: s.m. Seedling of a mango-plant. (It is rubbed down till an opening is made, and is blown, like a pipe or whistle, by children.)

ety: S. आम्र+क+dim. aff. र; Pr. अम्बो+लको; — o-lā is a com. dim. aff. in the modern Aryan tongues
def: s.m. Seedling of a mango-plant. (It is rubbed down till an opening is made, and is blown, like a pipe or whistle, by children.)
s.m. = amāwaṭ, q.v.
s.m. A beginner, a tyro).
v.n. = ān-paṛnā, q.v.s.v. ānā.
v.n. To come to hand.
s.m. Order, command; (in Gram.) the imperative mood; — affair, business, transaction; event, occurrence; fact, circumstance; matter, case, thing, particular; point, question
subs: s.m. Commands and prohibitions, orders and counter-orders.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَمْر (amr), from Arabic أَمْر (ʔamr).

def: s.m. The hog-plum, Spondias mangifera. (Also written amārā, ambāṛā, and amṛā.)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُمَرَاء (ʔumarāʔ).

def: s.m. The mango tree. (See ām).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَمْر (amr), from Arabic أَمْر (ʔamr).

def: s.m. The mango tree. (See ām).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آمر (āmir), from Arabic آمِر (ʔāmir).

def: adj. & s.m. Beardless; handsome; a beardless, handsome youth.
s.f. = ām-rāʼī, q.v.s.v. īm.
s.f. A mango-grove ( = ām-rāʼī and ambrāʼī, q.q.v.).
s.m. = ām-ras, q.v.s.v. ām.
s.m. pl. (of marẓ), Sicknesses, diseases, ailments.
s.m. The nectar conferring immortality; ambrosia, the drink of the gods, the water of life; anything sweet; any pleasant drink; a guava; immortality; final emancipation
subs: imrat-bān (amr̤it+vān), s.m. A variety of the plantain (kelā); a glazed or china jar ( = martabān)
v.t. To sprinkle with the water of life, to restore to life.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अमृत (amṛta). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi امرت (amrt).

def: s.m. The nectar conferring immortality; ambrosia, the drink of the gods, the water of life; anything sweet; any pleasant drink; a guava; immortality; final emancipation
subs: imrat-bān (amr̤it+vān), s.m. A variety of the plantain (kelā); a glazed or china jar ( = martabān)
v.t. To sprinkle with the water of life, to restore to life.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अमृत (amṛta). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi امرت (amrt).

def: s.f. Immortality, deathlessness.
v.s.v. amr̤it.
s.f. A small vessel for drinking out of; a kind of sweetmeat made of pulse (resembling the jalebī); a kind of small and sweet melon (local); a kind of cloth; — adj. Nectarious.
s.m. The guava, Psidium pyriferum, or pomiferum. (In Persian amrūd is also applied to the pear.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian امرود (amrūd).

def: s.m. The guava, Psidium pyriferum, or pomiferum. (In Persian amrūd is also applied to the pear.)
s.m. 'Amr and Zaid (fictitious names, like our 'John Nokes and Thomas Styles,' used largely in Mohammadan legal and scholastic discussions).
s.f. pl. (of mas̤al), Equals; likenesses, resemblances; proverbs, adages.
s.m. pl. (of miṣr), Large cities: cities.
(for A. امثلة), s.f. pl. (of mis̤āl), Exemplars, examples.
s.m. See ām.
s.m. A mango ( = ām).
s.f. Playing or fighting with clubs, cudgelling
subs: (-par), To drop or draw a perpendicular (on)
s.m. The morning rays or refulgence
or ʻamūd nikālnā, To erect a perpendicular (on, -par), to draw a perpendicular (from, -se).

def: adv. Perpendicularly.
adj. Such a one, a certain person, so-and-so; such-and-such ( = fulāṅ).
adj. Not chief; inferior, ordinary.
adj. Without root, destitute of origin or foundation.
adj. Formless, shapeless; unembodied.

ety: of āmurzīdan; ā+rt. Zend marez; S. marj, rt. मृज्
def: s.m. He who pardons; an epithet of God.
s.f. Pardon, grace; forgiveness of sins (by God).
s.f. pl. (of mauj, q.v.), Waves, billows.
s.m. pl. (of māl), Goods, chattels, effects, possessions, property; riches, wealth.
s.m. See s.v. ām.
s.f. pl. (of maut, q.v.), Deaths; — s.m. pl. (of maiyit), The dead.
adj. = ān and anya, q.q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

ety: S. अयन, अयान; and cf. मान and आज्ञा; Prk. आणा or आना
def: s.f. Course, way, manner, mode ( = andāz, ḍhang, t̤aur); natural disposition or temperament; habit, peculiarity; way or manner of a belle or a coquette, gracefulness, grace, elegance, charm, blandishment ( = adā); affectation; bashfulness, modesty; conceit, pride; will, pleasure, wish; rank, dignity, respectability, reputation, character; proper spirit, self-respect; established rule or custom; vow, oath; promise; interdicted thing; objection, scruple; hindrance, prohibition
subs: s.f. Way, fashion; habit, peculiarity; grace, elegance, beauty; pride, conceit; spirit, proper pride, dignity
(-kī), To violate or infringe the established rule or custom (of), to break away (from); to violate an oath or a vow; to break a promise
(-kī), To recognize the rank or dignity (of), to acknowledge the superiority of; to confess oneself beaten or vanquished; to swear allegiance to.

def: s.f. Order, command ( = āggyā, and ḥukm).
s.f. Time; moment, instant, second
subs: adv. In a second, in an instant, in a trice ( = ānaṉ-fānaṉ, q.v.).

def: prep. From; of; for, in lieu of; with; on, upon, above, over; before; after; because of
subs: adjacent, near; impending, imminent; — nearly; soon, shortly; almost

def: A negative or privative prefix ( = अ a), corresponding to the English prefixes un-, in-. (In words purely Sanskrit it is prefixed only to those beginning with a vowel; but in Hindī its use is not so restricted, as will be seen from the numerous examples that follow.)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

def: adj. Other, &c. = S. anya, q.v.

ety: cf. H. ān, and S. anya
def: adv. In that place, there
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

def: s.m. Postponement; delay; opportune or seasonable moment; opportunity
subs: adj. Procrastinating, dilatory; time-serving; seeking opportunity; — s.m. Dilatory person; time-server.

ety: S. आणकः, or अणकः, 'inferior, small'; rt. अण्
def: s.m. A copper coin, the sixteenth part of a rupee; a measure of land equal to 1⁄640th of an acre; a sixteenth part or share in a business or property. (The term ānā is also used in estimating quality, character, and ability, — much as the expression 'Sixteen ounces to the pound' is used in England; — thus, a man is said to be about 12 ānās — i.e. as not quite up to the mark)
subs: s.m. A fee or levy of one anna per rupee.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀯𑁂𑀇 (āvei), from Sanskrit आपयति (āpayati), from आप् (āp) + -अयति (-ayati). The perfective form آیا (āyā) may be derived from Sanskrit आगत (ā́gata, “arrived”).

def: s.m. Coming, advent, arrival ( = āwan)
subs: s.m. Coming and going; intercourse; visiting; communication; interchange of visits.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀯𑁂𑀇 (āvei), from Sanskrit आपयति (āpayati), from आप् (āp) + -अयति (-ayati). The perfective form آیا (āyā) may be derived from Sanskrit आगत (ā́gata, “arrived”).

ety: S. आ+यानीयं, rt. या; or गमनीयं, rt. गम्; cf. the Hindī forms आवना and आमना
def: v.n. To come; to approach; to arrive, reach (to), attain (to); to be acquired; to know how, to be able ( = Fr. savoir); to come to pass, happen, betide, befall; to be or become; to appear; to grow, increase, swelled be inflamed; to rise, to overflow; to come (on, -meṅ), to fit; to be bent or set (upon, -par); to come on, come forth, to pass away, to pass (as urine, semen, &c.)
subs: ān-paṛnā (-par), To come or fall (upon), come down, descend (upon); to fall (to), to be acquired, to accrue (to); to befall, betide; to present oneself or itself
v.n. To come or draw near, to come (to), arrive (at)
v.n. To come, &c. ( = ānā); to come, or appear, suddenly or unexpectedly: ā-rahnā, ān-rahnā, v.n. To fall or come down (from, -se); to come and reside or dwell (in, -meṅ)
v.n. To fall down ( = gir paṛnā); to fall (upon); to press, throng, make a rush (at or upon)
v.n. To be close upon, be close at hand; to beset, to fall (upon), lie in wait for
ān-lenā, v.t. To come up to, to reach, to overtake
ān-milnā (-se), To come in contact (with), to meet (with), unite (with)
(-par), To happen (to), to be afflicted (by), be overtaken (by)

v.n. To chance to come, to come suddenly or unexpectedly, to turn up. (The root of ānā, in the sense of 'to happen,' and sometimes of 'with,' is commonly prefixed to other neuter verbs of motion. Compare with this use of it that of the French verb venir 'to come.' Ánā is sometimes added to a verbal base to intensify it, e.g. uṭh-ānā, nikāl-ānā, To rise suddenly; to escape away, to come right out. It is also used to form statical verbs: e.g. rote ānā, 'to come weeping.')
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀯𑁂𑀇 (āvei), from Sanskrit आपयति (āpayati), from आप् (āp) + -अयति (-ayati). The perfective form آیا (āyā) may be derived from Sanskrit आगत (ā́gata, “arrived”).

def: s.m. The sixteenth part of a rupee = ānā, q.v.
s.m. The pubes; the groin.
s.f. Difficulty, distress, trouble, labour; fatigue, weariness (syn. ranj).
adj. Unbarred, without restraint, free; self-willed; licentious.
s.m. The egg plant and its fruit, Solanum melongena. (The common name is baigan).
adj. Not aware, ignorant.
s.f. Ignorance, stupidity.
s.m. Absence of desire, indifference.
adj. Lacking desire, indifferent; — s.m. One who is free from desire.

adj. Not given to religious observances; regardless of custom, law or propriety; irreligious; unprincipled; ill-behaved; indecorous, indecent; — s.m. One who is not given to religious observances; an unprincipled man, &c.; — neglect or disregard of religious observances; irreligion, unprincipled or improper conduct, indecorum.
adj. = an-āćār, q.v.
adv. = an-ćit, q.v.
s.f. Thoughtlessness, inattention; ignorance, stupidity.
s.f. Thoughtlessness, inattention; ignorance, stupidity.
adj. Without beginning; eternal; immemorial; unborn, uncreated
subs: s.f. Land that has been waste from time immemorial

def: s.m. Disrespect, disregard, irreverence; disparagement; indignity, affront, slight
adj. Not to be honoured or venerated; disreputable.
adj. Not subject, not dependent, independent.
adj. Without beginning; eternal; immemorial; unborn, uncreated
subs: s.f. Land that has been waste from time immemorial

def: s.m. Eternity without beginning.
part. Unhonoured disregarded.
adj. Inaccessible, unapproachable; unattainable, unobtainable.
part. adj. (rt. gam). Not come, not arrived; future; not attained, not learnt; unknown.
adj. Sinless, guiltless, blameless, innocent.
s.f. Allowance or discount of one anna in the rupee.
s.m. Absence of pride, humility.
adj. Not proud, humble; — s.m. A humble person.
s.m. Abstinence from food, fasting, starvation.
adj. Fasting; — s.m. One who fasts.
s.m. Want of union, discord, contention.
adj. Useless, worthless, bad.
adv. Easily, &c. (see an-āyās).
s.m. Grain, corn; food
subs: s.m. lit. 'Enemy of grain'; a lazy fellow, an idler
anāj-kā koṭhlā, s.m. Grain-worm, weevil; — one who subsists on corn (as man).

def: s.m. A large military drum beaten at one end, a kettle-drum.
s.f. Turning a deaf ear; overlooking, winking or conniving at; fighting shy of; shyness, backwardness, reserve
subs: v.t. To turn a deaf ear (to); to overlook, wink or connive at, pay no heed (to), disregard, feign blindness (to); make light of; fight shy of; to pretend, make excuses; to evade, shuffle

def: s.f. Turning a deaf ear; overlooking, winking or conniving at; fighting shy of; shyness, backwardness, reserve
subs: v.t. To turn a deaf ear (to); to overlook, wink or connive at, pay no heed (to), disregard, feign blindness (to); make light of; fight shy of; to pretend, make excuses; to evade, shuffle

def: s.f. = anākrāntā, q.v.
adj. Without form, shapeless.
adv. Without cause or occasion, groundlessly, uselessly, needlessly.
s.f. Angry look; displeasure, anger; envy, malignity, malice, wickedness.
s.f. Prickly nightshade, Solanum jacquini.
adj. Unable to read, illiterate; — s.m. An illiterate person, ignoramus, dolt, blockhead.
s.f. (but m. in S.), Fire; flame; — the plant Plumbago zeylanica and rosea.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अनल m (anala, “fire”).

def: coll. subst. m. pl. Mankind, mortals; creatures ( = ḵẖalq); jinn; demons.
s.m. The ring finger.
s.f. (by metathesis) for amānat, q.v.
adj. Healthy; unhurt, uninjured; — s.m. (f.?) Healthiness, health.
s.f. The ring finger ( = anāmā, q.v.).
s.m. pl. (of anmila), Fingers; tips of the fingers.
s.m. Mouth, face, visage.
s.f. Order, command ( = ān, q.v.).
demons. pron. pl. (of āṅ), Those; they

s.m. Mirth, merriment, revelry; rapture; comfort
adj. Blissful, blessed; — s.m. An epithet of the Deity
To be happy, to be delighted (with, -se), to derive pleasure (from); — ānand ho, Are you happy? Are you well? How do you do?
wety: From Sanskrit आनन्द (ānanda).

def: s.f. Happiness, &c. = ānand, q.v.
adj. Happy, joyful, cheerful; — s.m. A happy or cheerful person.
part. Pleased, delighted, joyful, cheerful.

ety: i.e. ānaṉ-fa-ānaṉ; ānaṉ, acc. of ān, q.v.
def: adv. Every moment, constantly, incessantly
subs: adv. In a moment, instantly = ān-kī-ān-meṅ, q.v.s.v. ān.

def: s.m. An epithet of Kāmdeva, the Hindū Cupid (lit. 'bodiless,' because he was reduced to ashes by a glance of Śiva's eye for having disturbed his devotions and rendered him enamoured of Pārvatī)
subs: s.m. 'The enemy of Anang or Kāmdeva'; an epithet of Śiva.

ety: for A. انانية, abst. subst. from anā, 'I'
def: s.f. Egotism, boasting, arrogance, self-conceit. (Syn. ahaṅkār.)
s.m. The pine-apple = ānanās, q.v.
wety: From Portuguese ananás.

def: adj. Of or relating to the pineapple; made like a pineapple; pineapple-shaped.
s.m. One who has no share; one who is excluded from a share of an inheritance on account of some legal flaw.

adj. Endless, boundless, infinite, eternal; — s.m. An epithet of the serpent that supports the earth; — an epithet of the god Vishnu; — a cord with fourteen knots which the Hindūs tie on the right arm on the festival of anant-ćaudas
subs: (S. ananta+ćaturdaśī), s.f. The fourteenth lunar day (or full moon) of Bhādoṅ, when a festival is held in honour of Anant or Vishnu
s.m. Eternity; — adv. Eternally. for ever and ever
s.m. Rigidity or paralysis of the muscles of the face and neck.

def: adj. Having no interstice, or interval, or pause; — postpn. Immediately (upon or after, -ke)
s.f. Endlessness, boundlessness, eternity, without ending.
adj. Endless, unbounded, infinite; — s.m. Endlessness, boundlessness, infinity.
adj. No other, no one else; one alone.
adj. Without measure
subs: anāp-śanāp, adj. & adv. With or without measure, beyond measure, excessive; immeasurably, immoderately, excessively.

def: adj. Unoffending, guiltless. &c. ( = the next, q.v.); — s.m. Guiltlessness, blamelessness, innocence.
adj. Unoffending, faultless, guiltless, blameless, innocent; — s.m. An unoffending or innocent person.
s.m. Non-acquaintance.
adj. Having no progeny, childless.
adj. Imperishable, indestructible; unfailing, enduring.
adj. = an-apeksh, q.v.
adj. Disregarding, neglecting; — s.m. A negligent person.
s.f. Disregard, heedlessness, carelessness, indifference.
s.m. The pomegranate, Punica granatum; a kind of firework (shaped like a pomegranate)
subs: s.m. A superior species of pomegranate without seeds (brought from Cabul and Persia)
s.m. Pomegranate seed (used in the digestive termed pāćak); a species of millet (so called from its resemblance to the seed of the pomegranate); a kind of red checked cloth (worn by women).
ex: آج کل انار مہنگے ہو گئے ہیں
āj kal anār mahaṅge ho gae ha͠i.
These days, pomegranates have become costly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَنَار (anār).

def: adj. Not easily discovered, secret.
adj. Of or relating to, or resembling the pomegranate.
adj. & s.m. = anāṛī, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Unskilful, awkward, clumsy, inexpert; inexperienced, unpractised, raw; uncouth, low, vulgar; uninformed, illiterate, ignorant; — novice, bungler, recruit, greenhorn; lout; blockhead, fool, idiot.
s.m. Awkwardness, clumsiness, inexpertness, unskilfulness, uncouthness; bungling; ignorance, stupidity, silliness.
s.m. = anāṛī-pan, q.v
s.m. Awkwardness, clumsiness, inexpertness, unskilfulness, uncouthness; bungling; ignorance, stupidity, silliness.
s.m. = anāṛī-pan, q.v.
s.m. Stage, theatre; war; name of a country and its inhabitants in the peninsula of Gujerāt; name of the capital (Dwārika) of Gujerāt.

adj. Meaningless, nonsensical, absurd; causeless, groundless; worthless, useless, purposeless, profitless, fruitless, vain, abortive; improper; detrimental, injurious; — s.m. Absurdity; disadvantage; misfortune; wrong, injustice, injury, oppression, tyranny; transgression, offence, guilt, sin
subs: To do injustice (to), to injure, oppress, do wrong (to); to commit an offence, to transgress, to sin.

def: adj. = an-arth, q v.
s.m. A wrong-doer, injurer, oppressor, cruel person; a mischief-maker; sinner, transgressor.
s.m. Abstinence from food, fasting.
s.f. = uns, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of ʻunṣuṛ, q.v.), The elements (of which material substances of different natures are composed).
adj. Not spiteful, not envious; — s.m. A person who is free from a spirit of envy and detraction.
adj. Imperishable, indestructible, immortal.
adj. Indestructible, imperishable.
s.m. Injustice, grievous oppression.
adv. Somewhere else, elsewhere.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अन्त (anta).

def: s.f. Twist, turn, fold; a peculiar fold of the dhotī (q.v.); entanglement; knot, joint (of a finger, &c. cf. aṅṭī, āṅtī); mark or scratch made on silver or gold to test its purity; opposition, contrariety, antagonism, enmity; grudge; envy; jar; misunderstanding (cf. ainṭ, anṭī)
subs: (-meṅ), To become knotted or entangled; to have a feeling of antagonism, enmity, envy, &c., to be at enmity (with), have a grudge (against); to have a misunderstanding or difference (with)
anṭ-kī sanṭ, adj. Irrelevant, unsuitable, inapplicable, inappropriate
s.f. Combination, league, intrigue, plot; a rambling or irrelevant speech; an evasive answer.

def: adj. Without lord, protector, husband, master or owner; husbandless, protectorless, fatherless; poor, helpless, forlorn; — s.m. A friendless or a destitute person; widow; orphan
subs: s.m. Land without an owner which is lying uncultivated, unclaimed waste.

def: adj. Pertaining to the unprotected, &c.
adj. Not having subdued self or one's own mind.
adj. Free from suffering or sickness, healthy, vigorous; — adv. Well.
s.m. A ring, generally furnished with little bells, worn by women on the great toes (cf. angūṭhā).
adj. Inadvertent; inattentive, careless; — s.m. Inadvertency; inattention, carelessness, indifference.
s.f. Inattention, &c. = an-avadhān, q.v.
adj. Without limit, unlimited, boundless, endless, interminable, not terminated.
adj. Blameless, faultless; not to be despised, honourable, distinguished.
adj. Having no opportunity; without occasion, uncalled for.
adj. & adv. Incessant; incessantly.
adj. Busy, having no leisure; — s.m. Want of leisure; inopportuneness, unseasonableness.
s.f. The absence of a fixed state, instability, unsteadiness, wavering, vacillation; doubt, uncertainty; incontinence.
adj. Unstable, changeable; incontinent.
part. adj. = an-avastha, q.v.
s.f. Want of rain, drought.
part. adj. Uncovered; undressed; uninclosed; bare; open.
adj. Having no leader or ruler, without a chief or protector; disorderly.
s.m. Absence of effort or exertion, ease, facility; — adj. & adv. Easy, ready; — easily, freely, readily; involuntarily.
= an-āyās, q.v.
s.m. Mango ( = ām, q.v.).
s.m. Discord, disagreement, difference, misunderstanding, dissension, jar, quarrel, dispute; dislike, enmity.
s.m. Discord, disagreement, difference, misunderstanding, dissension, jar, quarrel, dispute; dislike, enmity.
part. adj. Unpierced, unbored, unperforated ( = an-bindhā).
s.m. Ill-luck, bad fortune.
adj. (f. -ī), Not good, bad.
adj. = a-bhay, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Distasteful, disagreeable, unpleasant, displeasing, repugnant, hateful, odious; — s.m. A disagreeable person, &c.
adj. Unpierced, &c. = an-bedhā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Unmarried; — s.m. An unmarried man, a bachelor.
s.f. An unmarried girl, virgin, spinster.
adj. (f. -ā), Speechless; silent dumb ( = abolā)
subs: s.f. Lit 'A silent queen'; a proud, or vain, woman.

def: adj. Unsown, unplanted; uncultivated; wild; spontaneous.
adj. Not knowing or understanding; ignorant, witless; unintentional.
s.f. Flame, blaze; flash, brilliance, splendour; heat, warmth; fire; maternal affection; passion, lust; burden, trial, difficulty
subs: prov. Fire toucheth not (has no effect upon) truth, truth has nothing to fear. (The proverb is based on the ordeal by fire.)

def: part. adj. Not desired.
s.m. The border or hem of a cloak, veil, shawl, or mantle; a kind of sheet or wrapper; the breasts or bosom (of a woman), teats (of an animal)
subs: s.m. The embroidered hem of a woman's sheet or mantle
(or āṅćar) dabānā or dābnā, To take, or to suck, the breast (a child)
(or āṅćar) denā (-ko), To give the breast (to an infant)

(par), To throw the sheet (over the head): āṅćal lenā (-kā), To touch the border or hem of a guest's garment as a sign of welcome; to wipe the hands with the sheet of the bride's or bridegroom's mother (a ceremony in marriages)
To tie a knot in the mantle or sheet (by way of reminder); to bear in mind, keep in memory.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अञ्चल (añcala).

def: s.m. The border or hem of a cloak, veil, shawl, or mantle; a kind of sheet or wrapper; the breasts or bosom (of a woman), teats (of an animal)
subs: s.m. The embroidered hem of a woman's sheet or mantle
(or āṅćar) dabānā or dābnā, To take, or to suck, the breast (a child)
(or āṅćar) denā (-ko), To give the breast (to an infant)

(par), To throw the sheet (over the head): āṅćal lenā (-kā), To touch the border or hem of a guest's garment as a sign of welcome; to wipe the hands with the sheet of the bride's or bridegroom's mother (a ceremony in marriages)
To tie a knot in the mantle or sheet (by way of reminder); to bear in mind, keep in memory.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀁𑀘𑀮 (aṃcala), from Sanskrit अञ्चल (añcala).

def: s.m. = āṅćal, q.v. cf. also aćrā and aćlā.
s.m. Valuation of a standing crop (syn. kan-kūt).
s.f. = āṅć, q.v.
s.m. A mystical or magical word or formula; spell, charm
subs: (-ke) To enthrall, ruin or destroy by pronouncing a spell (over), to bring under one's influence by means of a spell.

def: part. adj. Untouched; unmolested.
part. adj. Unpared, unpeeled; unsmoothed, unpolished, rough, rude, uncivilized, awkward, uncouth, boorish.
adj. Inattentive, heedless, careless; unconscious; absent-minded; — anćittā, s.m. A slap or thump given unawares from behind
subs: an-ćitte-meṅ, adv. Unconsciously; unwittingly; unawares, unexpectedly, suddenly, all at once; in an unguarded moment.

def: adj. Inattentive, heedless, careless; unconscious; absent-minded; — anćittā, s.m. A slap or thump given unawares from behind
subs: an-ćitte-meṅ, adv. Unconsciously; unwittingly; unawares, unexpectedly, suddenly, all at once; in an unguarded moment.

def: s.m. The border or hem of a cloak, veil, shawl, or mantle; a kind of sheet or wrapper; the breasts or bosom (of a woman), teats (of an animal)
subs: s.m. The embroidered hem of a woman's sheet or mantle
(or āṅćar) dabānā or dābnā, To take, or to suck, the breast (a child)
(or āṅćar) denā (-ko), To give the breast (to an infant)

(par), To throw the sheet (over the head): āṅćal lenā (-kā), To touch the border or hem of a guest's garment as a sign of welcome; to wipe the hands with the sheet of the bride's or bridegroom's mother (a ceremony in marriages)
To tie a knot in the mantle or sheet (by way of reminder); to bear in mind, keep in memory.

def: s.m. = āṅćal, q.v. cf. also aćrā and aćlā.
s.m. The raspberry, Rubus paniculatus or tiliaceus; blackberry, bramble, Rubus fruticosus (in Kāngra āṅćū is termed ākhi = S. ākshika. Other dialectic names are aṅkrī, ālać, kanāći)
adv. Even so, even as, just as.
v.t. To cause or make (one) rinse the mouth; to supply (one) with water to rinse the mouth with.
v.t. To rinse or cleanse the mouth after eating &c. = aćānā, q.v.
s.m. The castor-oil tree, Palma Christi, Ricinus communis. (See anḍī.)
s.m. The castor-oil tree, Palma Christi, Ricinus communis. (See anḍī.)
s.m. = āṅdan, q.v.
s.m. Egg (see anḍā); testicle (see āṅṛ)
subs: adj. & s.m. Egg-shaped, oval, elliptical, ellipsoidal; — oval, ellipse, ellipsoid
s.m. Swelling of the testicles or scrotum, hydrocele
s.m. The shell of the mundane egg; the world
s.m. Scrotum; musk-bag (of the musk-deer); — rupture, hernia

def: s.m. Egg; a kind of fire-work (so called because the composition is put into an egg-shell)
subs: (-kā), lit. 'One's egg to become addled'; to be reduced to a pitiful condition; to become thin, wasted, or weak; to be wearied, jaded, or worn out; to fail in business, become insolvent
s.m. Wife and children, family; children, chicks, brood
To sit on eggs, to brood, incubate, hatch; to keep oneself secluded, lead a retired life
s.m. An inexperienced youth, simpleton, greenhorn.

def: s.m. = āṅdan, q.v.
s.m. Egg (see anḍā); testicle (see āṅṛ)
subs: adj. & s.m. Egg-shaped, oval, elliptical, ellipsoidal; — oval, ellipse, ellipsoid
s.m. Swelling of the testicles or scrotum, hydrocele
s.m. The shell of the mundane egg; the world
s.m. Scrotum; musk-bag (of the musk-deer); — rupture, hernia

def: adj. Oviparous ( = anḍaj); with egg; laying (fowl).
adj. Produced from an egg, oviparous; — s.m. An oviparous animal; bird, fish, snake, lizard.
adj. Little, small, few; — andake, A little; a few.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. anḍa.

ety: perf. part. of andāḵẖtan; Zend hām+rt. tać; S. सम्+तञ्च
def: part. Thrown, cast; formed; cast away.
s.f. The nightingale (syn. hazār dāstān).

ety: Zend handāma, rt. dā, 'to set' = S. धा
def: s.m. Body, figure, stature; member
s.m. Mud, mire; quagmire ( = āṅd, andā, q.q.v.).
s.m. Narrative, story, tale, fiction.
prep. Within, in the inside (of, -ke)
subs: v.t. To put within, put out of sight; to put into, insert; to penetrate; to thrust or push in
v.n. To be contained or included (in, -ke), to belong (to), fall under; to enter, go in.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اندر (andar, “inside, within”).

def: s.m. A kind of sweetmeat made of rice and flour formed into balls, then fried in ghī and covered with sugar; a kind of cloth: — andarse-kī goliyāṅ = andarse.
adj. Within; — s.m. Interior, inside; inner apartments.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَنْدَرُون (andarūn)

def: adj. Interior, internal, inner; intrinsic (see bhītarī).
adj. Interior, internal, inner; intrinsic (see bhītarī).
s.m. Precept, advice, admonition; last will or advice.

r: andāz, andāḵẖtan, andāḵẖta, barq-andāz, lit, gol-andāz
def: part. act. (of andāḵẖtan; see andāḵẖta), Throwing; thrower, caster, shooter; (used as last member of comp. e.g. barq-andāz, lit. 'lightning-thrower'; a matchlock-man
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian انداز (andāz).

def: s.m. Measure, measurement; quantity; weighing, weight; degree, amount; valuing, valuation, value; rough estimate; conjecture, guess; proportion, symmetry; elegance, grace; mode, manner, style, fashion, pattern; carriage, bearing, gait; modulation (of the voice); time (in music)
subs: (-kā), To copy the manner or fashion (of), to imitate; to take off, mimic
s.f. Estimate of the value of a standing crop ( = kan-kūt)
s.f. A highly adorned or painted beauty; a vain, affected woman
(or andāze-) se, adv. In due measure or proportion; — approximately, by guess
(or andāza) karnā (-kā), To judge (of), to estimate the value (of), to value; to estimate roughly; to guess or conjecture.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian انداز (andāz).

def: s.m. Measure, measurement; quantity; weighing, weight; degree, amount; valuing, valuation, value; rough estimate; conjecture, guess; proportion, symmetry; elegance, grace; mode, manner, style, fashion, pattern; carriage, bearing, gait; modulation (of the voice); time (in music)
subs: (-kā), To copy the manner or fashion (of), to imitate; to take off, mimic
s.f. Estimate of the value of a standing crop ( = kan-kūt)
s.f. A highly adorned or painted beauty; a vain, affected woman
(or andāze-) se, adv. In due measure or proportion; — approximately, by guess
(or andāza) karnā (-kā), To judge (of), to estimate the value (of), to value; to estimate roughly; to guess or conjecture.

ety: from andāz, after the model of the Ar. acc.
def: adv. By guess, conjecturally; approximately, about ( = andāz-se and aṭkal-se).
s.m. pl. Strolls, rambles
subs: To take a stroll, to ramble, rove.

def: part. adj. Unseen; invisible; undiscovered, undetected.
part. adj. Unseen; invisible; undiscovered, undetected.

ety: of andeshīdan 'to reflect'; Zend prep. ham+rt. du; S. सम्+ध्वन्?
def: s.m. = andesha, q.v.
subs: andesh-nāk, adj. Thoughtful, pensive; anxious, concerned, full of anxiety; — andeshmand hona (-se), To be anxious or concerned (about); to take thought (of)

def: s.m. Thought, consideration, meditation, reflection; solicitude, anxiety, concern (syn. ćintā and fikr); doubt, misgiving, suspicion; apprehension, dread, fear; danger, peril, (syn. ḵẖat̤ra)
subs: (-kā), To feel anxious (about), feel anxiety or concern (for); to be apprehensive (of)
adj. = andesh-nāk, adj. Thoughtful, &c. ( = andesh-nāk, q.v.); dangerous, perilous, hazardous.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Blind, stone-blind; benighted; undiscerning; dark, obscure, dim; — s.m. A blind man
subs: v.t. To blind, &c. = andhā-karnā, q.v.; to make one appear blind, impute blindness to

s.m. lit. 'Blind buffalo': a boys' game (a boy mounts on the back of another who is blindfolded and leans against a wall while several other boys pass under him, and he is required to name the first of these)
s.m. A corrupt court, government, or office; misgovernment (see andhī sarkār)

adj. Blinding; blind; indiscriminate; excessive, violent, outrageous; rash, precipitate, wild; — s.m. Blindness; rashness, wildness; violence, excess, outrage, commotion, disturbance, pother; misgovernment, anarchy; — adv. Blindly; indiscriminately; wildly, rashly; excessively, violently, outrageously; — andhā-dhund uṭhānā, To raise a commotion, make a stir or pother; to do violence or outrage; to raise an outcry; to waste, dissipate, squander; — andhā-dhund ronā, v.n. To weep violently or bitterly
s.f. A fearful cry; a cry for help or redress; — anāhā-dhund lutānā, To squander outrageously, spend most extravagantly
s.f. A blind government, a bad or corrupt government; mal-administration, gross misgovernment; a bad paymaster, one who keeps his subordinates in arrears
v.t. To blind, put out the eyes of; to blindfold; to hoodwink; to deceive
andhā-kūp, s.m. A well of which the mouth is hidden, or which is overgrown with plants, &c., or which is choked with rubbish
To be or become blind; to lose one's sight, be blinded (by); to be blind (to), to shut (one's) eyes (to), to connive or wink (at).

def: adj. Blind, dark ( = andhā, q.v.); — s.m. Blindness; darkness
subs: andh-kār, s.m. Darkness (see andhiyārā, the com. form); a violent storm of wind and dust (severer than āndhī, q.v.)

def: v.s.v. andhā; and see dhundh.
adj. Blind; — s.m. A blind man (see andhā).
s.m. see s.v. ann.
s.m. Blindness; darkness; purblindness; short-sightedness; intellectual blindness; ignorance; folly; rashness.
s.m. A violent dust-storm (see andh-kār, s.v. andh).
s.f. Blindness, &c. (see andhāpan).
s.f. Heat-rash, prickly heat (cf. ambhaurī and ghamaurī).
s.m. = andhāpan, q.v.
s.m. Darkness, gloom; violence, outrage, wrong, injustice, iniquity; tyranny, oppression; lawlessness, misrule, anarchy
subs: (-par), To commit an outrage (on), to outrage; to do violence (to); to tyrannize (over), to oppress, to treat with cruelty; to act lawlessly

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Dark, obscure, sombre, dim; — darkness, obscurity, gloom, shadow; cloudiness; haze, mist
subs: To keep out of the light (of anyone)
adj. Somewhat dark; very dark; sombre, shady, dusky; hazy, misty
v.t. To make dark, to darken, obscure, overshadow, cloud; to cast a shade or gloom over; to extinguish (a light); to obstruct light, stand in the light of
adv. When the darkness is illumined by the moon, &c.; late at night and early dawn; at twilight
s.f. Dark chamber; black hole; (met.) stomach; womb; secret chamber; mysteries of nature; mystery, enigma; — andherī koṭhrī-kā yār, s.m. A secret lover
s.m. lit. 'The light of a dark house,' an only son; a very beautiful child or person
adv. lit. 'When the face is indistinguishable owing to darkness'; when there is not sufficient light to see clearly, at the first dawn of day, very early in the morning (also muṅh-andhere).

def: s.f. Darkness, obscurity; blind or blinkers for a horse; a horse covering which also covers the eyes
subs: v.n. To be overcast; to lower; darkness to set in
andherī ḍālnā, v.t. To blindfold; to blind, hoodwink, deceive, impose upon.
